findings.complexity: O(R) full-file reads; each file O(bytes of entire run JSON)
findings.threshold: R>20 or any run file >~500KB (many results per run); multi-MB histories become slow quickly
findings.impact: Every GET /runs reads and json.loads the complete body of each run_*.json (including full results arrays). Listing history does not need per-result payloads, so CPU, memory, and latency scale with total stored history size, not row count shown.
findings.fix: Store sidecar metadata files (e.g. run_{id}.meta.json) written at run end, or read only first N KB / stream-parse top-level keys; alternatively SQLite/index for list view and load full run only on GET /runs/{id}.

findings.complexity: O(N) sequential read_text per script; O(total bytes) memory for content strings
findings.threshold: N>~50 or scripts >~20KB each; noticeable API latency on HDD or large repos
findings.impact: For each *.py file the handler reads the entire file to compute lines, preview[:500], and heuristic case_id scan. One list request triggers N full disk reads; response payload also embeds previews for all scripts.
findings.fix: Use f.stat() for size; read only first ~2–4KB for header/heuristic; omit preview from list and fetch on row expand or GET /scripts/{name}; optional asyncio.gather or thread pool for parallel stat/head reads.

findings.complexity: O(N) string concat via map+join; single innerHTML write; esc() is O(N*fields) extra DOM createElement churn
findings.threshold: N>~300–800 rows typical for main-thread jank; 1000+ often causes visible freeze on mid devices
findings.impact: Assigning a huge HTML string to tbody.innerHTML blocks the main thread during parse and layout. filterCases calls renderCases on filtered set so large datasets re-render whole tables. esc() allocates a temporary DOM node per field per row.
findings.fix: Virtualize the table (e.g. windowed rows), paginate server-side, or use DocumentFragment incremental updates; replace esc with a string-only escape for hot paths; yield chunk rendering via requestAnimationFrame.

findings.complexity: O(N) for table innerHTML + O(N) summary loop; no per-file fetch in browser
findings.threshold: N>~500 rows or very wide mapped_cases cells; usually network-bound from list_scripts payload size
findings.impact: The SPA does not read script files locally; it calls GET /scripts once. Cost is dominated by backend full-file reads and JSON size (previews), then one large innerHTML assign for the table.
findings.fix: Backend slim list response; frontend virtualization if row count grows; lazy script detail fetch (already partial via editScript GET).

findings.complexity: O(1) HTTP + O(R) DOM rows from map+join innerHTML
findings.threshold: DOM side: R>~200 rows; real cost usually server list_runs reading huge JSON files
findings.impact: Client does not parse run files; it only renders the run list returned by the API. Sequential file parsing happens on the server in list_runs.
findings.fix: Same as list_runs backend fix; optionally cap displayed runs or paginate the API.

findings.complexity: O(M) innerHTML for M results in one selected run
findings.threshold: M>~1000 test results in a single run
findings.impact: After one GET /runs/{id}, builds a large results table in one innerHTML assignment; can freeze UI for huge single-run payloads.
findings.fix: Virtualize execution result rows or paginate result list in the API.

findings.complexity: O(S) read_text over all scripts in heuristic fallback path
findings.threshold: S>~30 when mappings empty and heuristic scans whole scripts dir
findings.impact: If no authoritative mappings match, every *.py is read fully to search for case_id in content.
findings.fix: Prefer mappings only; constrain heuristic to filename/stem; or index case_id in metadata files at generation time.

findings.impact: Reads one small meta file per report; generally acceptable compared to run/script full reads.
findings.fix: Pagination or single index file if K grows very large.

findings.impact: Each filter pass allocates a new filtered array and rebuilds entire table HTML.
findings.fix: Debounce input; virtual list; or server-side query parameters.
